core_mechanism: second-order two-point statistics of a point pattern on a sphere — the angular two-point correlation function together with its spherical-harmonic (multipole) power spectrum — used to detect departures from statistical isotropy against a uniform-random null distribution
brief_reason: The method quantifies directional structure in 536 sky positions via the two-point angular correlation function and a sigma-map angular power spectrum (multipole decomposition), comparing both to the isotropic/uniform-random expectation.
